text:
Perplexity just launched “Perplexity Computer” which is an end-to-end AI system that can research, build & deploy projects autonomously.

Every AI lab is racing towards agentic systems that do end-to-end work. Super bullish for $MU since each new agent means more memory per GPU.
Wild to see $MELI trading at an all-time low forward valuation
$AXON UP OVER 20%
$JOBY breaking above $10 on the $UBER partnership announcement

$TSM Arizona fab buildout spans ~1,100 acres and is expected to cost ~$165B.

This is shaping up to be one of the most important U.S. infrastructure projects of the decade.

$ODD down over 40% after guiding Q1 revenue to decline ~30% versus the market expecting ~21% growth.

Management blamed algorithm changes that spiked CAC but when a distribution shift can drive this kind of miss… then that’s a structural issue that warrants a pre-announcement.

$META AI broke its traffic record in January by hitting ~23M visits.

The standalone site is still small versus ChatGPT but it’s meaningful progress when you consider Meta’s real distribution advantage is embedding AI across a ~4B-user ecosystem.

$UBER launches Uber Air with $JOBY electric air taxis enabling one-tap booking for eVTOL flights starting in Dubai later this year.

This reinforces Uber’s playbook of not building autonomy or aircraft but own the demand layer for whatever mobility platform actually scales.

Don't be confused by $MSFT ~30% drawdown.

The business is firing on all cylinders and is on track to generate ~$17B in operating profit per month by 2028.

That's over 10x the operating profit from a decade ago at a near decade-low multiple.

Absolutely wild that $AXON added ~$3B in contracted bookings in a single quarter.

To put that in perspective, Axon's entire contracted backlog was ~$7B less than two years ago.

Draft One and other AI tools are driving higher ACVs and longer contract terms as customers re-up.
$ZETA hitting profitability while guiding ~40% growth in Q1 is impressive.

$MELI five years ago:
• ~$1,900/share
• ~$21B GMV with ~$1.7B annual profit

$MELI today:
• ~$1,900/share
• ~$65B in GMV with ~$12.9B in annual profit

Same price. Very different business.

At the iConnections Global Alts Conference in Miami, gold is hardly ever discussed. If it comes up at all, it is usually dismissed as a relic or ignored entirely.  Not this year.  On a main stage panel featuring @VD718 Vincent Daniel of Seawolf Capital, Danny Moses of The Danny Moses Show @dmoses34 and On The Tape, and Meredith Whitney of Meredith Whitney Advisory Group, moderated by @GuyAdami  of @RiskReversal Media, gold was discussed positively and with conviction.  “Everybody should own some of it in some portion of their portfolio.”  “Gold is my therapist.”  They talked about debasement, structural deficits, central bank accumulation and policy, physical delivery constraints, the issues with paper gold and silver via ETFs.  China accumulating physical gold. They discussed silver shortages tied to EVs, solar, and data centers. All the topics we cover extensively @MilesFranklinCo   When gold moves from niche macro conversations to institutional panels at a global alts investment conference, that is a signal. Very bullish.

On Wall Street, many people want someone to "make a call."

I don't make calls. I listen. I listen to my market signals the same way a tracker reads broken branches, disturbed soil, and fresh prints in the dirt. The signal is already there. 

The only question is whether you're awake enough to hear it

📺 REPEATABLE RED DOG REVERSAL SETUPS THAT PAY

Yesterday, I had the best trade (in $MSFT) in months — not because of opinion, but because of structure.

Microsoft was a technically damaged stock. Most traders either got caught long and frustrated or avoided it entirely.

But the Red Dog Reversal (RDR) tactic offered a precise intraday opportunity in $MSFT that I go through in detail in this Short video. 

On the other side, $AAPL was a different type of RDR setup – a power reclaim trade, which I also cover in this Short video.

Both $MSFT and $AAPL showed that even the biggest stocks in the world respect technical structure. And when they do — if you're prepared and disciplined — you get paid.

Learn more about Red Dog Reversal here: nitter.net/RedDogT3/status/189632…

If you enjoyed this video, please ❤️like and 🔁retweet

$jpm did a tactical RDR around the 200day for active cash flow. There were many yesterday.  Trim if u held some - or just take the day and a half trade.  Same tactic happened in $MSFT and $hood. 
Learn it

📺 THE MARKET IS COILED AT RESISTANCE AHEAD OF $NVDA

$SPY is still trading within a clearly defined range, around $689, just below the $690 resistance. 

The key question today: do we clear $688.35–$690 before Nvidia earnings, or does the market stall?

Right now, the range is intact — but pressure is building toward that upper boundary.

$QQQ is also extremely tight. It lagged recently; software weakness weighed on it, but a tactical software bounce yesterday helped it stabilize.

Key level: above $604 → shorts had to step aside. Next resistance: $609.56 / $610.50. Upside target if momentum builds: $617.

Everything is compressing ahead of $NVDA earnings today. Nvidia earnings are the catalyst that likely determines whether we break higher or reject.

#NVDA chart is extremely tight — similar to last year before a major move.

This kind of compression often leads to: big expansion, high volatility, and options repricing.

I bought $195 calls for around $4. My plan is to trim if the price pushes toward $199.

If you enjoyed this video, please ❤️like and 🔁retweet
$nvda pre-earnings look as it does have a nice base

$SPX futures are up 18 after the range held again yesterday, with 6815 proving support. Now we see if it can clear 6916 and hold it ahead of $NVDA earnings tonight, with the top end of the range at 6993–7002.
